summary: Well-organized test suite with comprehensive coverage of all core requirements: manifest parsing (package.json, requirements.txt), dependency extraction, license compliance checking (approved/denied/unknown), CLI with correct exit codes, report formatting, and GitHub Actions workflow validation via actionlint and act. Tests are properly isolated with clean fixtures, effective mocking via mockLookup factory, and clear separation by module. Strengths: all main features exercised, good fixture quality, independent tests with proper cleanup, end-to-end workflow testing via act with exact output verification, and proper exit code testing. Weaknesses: limited edge case exploration (only basic operators tested in requirements.txt like ==, >=, not ~=, !=, <, >; no extras syntax or environment markers; malformed versions not tested); incomplete error path coverage (missing config file not tested, invalid JSON in config/licenses not tested, empty allow/deny lists untested); boundary conditions sparse (no tests for empty strings, special characters, very long inputs, duplicate entries); workflow pipeline tests only verify 'push' trigger, not schedule or workflow_dispatch. Would catch most regressions in core functionality but may miss bugs in version normalization edge cases, manifest parsing edge cases, or certain error handling paths.
